economic_notes: First Nation is pursuing three specific claims against the Crown: 1960 Surrender Claim, Surveyor Error and Roadway Claim, and Treaty Benefits Claim, financed through RBC credit facilities. First Nation owns 30 Wood Bison (biological assets). COVID-19 impacted operations including reduced gaming revenues. Cochin Convenience Centre is a band-owned business.
notes: Fiscal year 2021 (ended March 31, 2021). Province is Saskatchewan. Auditor identified from Management Responsibility section as MNP LLP (independent firm of Chartered Professional Accountants, Prince Albert, Saskatchewan). Consolidated entities include: Saulteaux First Nation CMHC Housing, Saulteaux First Nation Employment and Training Inc. (inactive), Saulteaux First Nation Enterprises Inc. (inactive), Soto Land Company Limited, Auski Land Corporation, and Cochin Convenience Centre. Three RBC claim loans totalling $3,061,838 classified as term loans due on demand. Emphasis of Matter paragraph in auditor report notes outstanding claims against Government of Canada financed by bank loans with insurance coverage. ISC funding calculated as ISC regular ($6,467,891) plus ISC Health ($2,087,205) = $8,555,096. Total government transfers include ISC, ISC Health, CMHC Subsidy, CMHC RRAP, BATC, FSIN, SITAG, TSEC. Restricted cash includes both current ($4,091,487) and long-term ($250,212). Annual debt service includes repayment of long-term debt ($301,612) plus capital lease repayments ($73,664) = $375,276. Prior year included a one-time contribution of sewage pumping station from Moosomin First Nation valued at $1,957,031.
